goal: Reference the existing soulmatch autonomy and anti-bureaucracy family as Bluepilot's autonomy governance anchor without creating a new charter or opening any activation surface.
scope_out: Do not create a new autonomy charter file, copy soulmatch documents into Bluepilot, add new runtime gates, routes, providers, tests, environment variables, deploy configuration, writes, persistence, activation, or external calls.
claims: Soulmatch already contains the canonical autonomy and anti-bureaucracy reference family.
Bluepilot should reference that family instead of creating a competing governance document.
The only behavior change is a documented review check against unnecessary limits, gates, approvals, or ceremony without a runnable artifact.
assumptions: The referenced soulmatch documents remain the current repo-family source until an AICOS-wide governance mirror exists.
A documentation anchor is sufficient for this block; activation and post-BPK-226 trimming require later human-gated tasks.
dod: AGENTS.md documents the soulmatch autonomy reference family and review check.
docs/CLAUDE-CONTEXT.md documents the same reference family and BPK-228 context.
docs/CODEX-RICHTUNGSBRIEF-optimized.md includes the Charter-Check for future blocks.
STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md record BPK-228 and its safety boundary.
No runtime, provider, write, route, test-machine, environment, deploy, or new charter file is added.
required_commands: rg -n "AI-AUTONOMY-LAYER|AI-TEAM-ANTI-BUREAUCRACY|AI-TEAM-AUTONOMY|MAYA-APPWIDE|Charter-Check" AGENTS.md docs/CLAUDE-CONTEXT.md docs/CODEX-RICHTUNGSBRIEF-optimized.md STATE.md docs/SESSION-LOG.md
stop_conditions: Creating a new charter, copying soulmatch docs, adding runtime gates/routes/tests, or changing provider, write, DB, auth, deploy, package, workflow, environment, or builder source files becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-227 merged to main at da3883752b077105d961ba7d59965abd6b6ec0e9. A repo-family check found that soulmatch already references AI-AUTONOMY-LAYER and AI-TEAM-ANTI-BUREAUCRACY as canonical policy documents, while Bluepilot only had an abstract charter slot and generic Charta wording.
